text.title: code-review-slo-and-rubric Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the code-review-slo-and-rubric methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Apply per team or per repo; never bundle multiple teams' rubrics into one artefact.
rule.rationale: Different teams have different SLO realities.

rule.statement: SLO values are sourced from current PR throughput data, not aspirational. Document the source dataset.
rule.rationale: Aspirational SLOs miss; sourced ones improve.

rule.statement: Named owner — one human or role-with-rotation. Never 'team' / 'we'.
Rubric distinguishes blockers from nits with a binary, observable criterion (not 'I prefer'). Nits are non-blocking by definition.
rule.rationale: Subjective blockers create reviewer-load + PR latency without quality gain.

rule.statement: AI pre-review is mechanical-only (style, obvious bugs, contract diffs). Judgment (design, security, invariants) is human-only.
rule.rationale: AI judgment on design quality is unreliable; human judgment on style is wasted.

rule.statement: Reviewer open-PR cap is enforced (CI bot or load-balancer); above the cap, the next assignment goes to a different reviewer.
rule.rationale: Above the cap, latency goes superlinear.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
